channel.title: Simple IT SRQ Blog
channel.description: Plain-English takes on security, AI, cloud, and compliance for Sarasota and Bradenton businesses.

item.title: AI Comprehension Debt: A Hidden Risk for SRQ IT Teams
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: AI coding assistants are shipping code faster than ever - but a senior engineer warns the real risk is teams losing track of what their software actually does. Heres what that means locally.

item.title: Gamified Tech Training: A Smarter Way to Onboard SRQ Staff
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: A viral browser game walks players through building a GPU from logic gates up. Its a sharp lesson in how serious technical training can hide inside something that feels like play.

item.title: AI Vendor Lock-In: Update Your Bradenton Procurement Playbook
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: Anthropic quietly blocked third-party tools from using paid Claude Code subscriptions. The move sparked a debate about lock-in - and a useful checklist for any business buying AI tooling.

item.title: LinkedIn Is Fingerprinting Your Browser. Heres What to Do.
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: Researchers found LinkedIns web client probing the users installed browser extensions for fingerprinting. Even boring SaaS apps are now running adversarial detection in production.

item.title: Evaluating Agentic Coding Tools: A Visual Primer for IT
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: A new diagrammed walkthrough of how Claude Code actually works under the hood is the best free primer for IT teams trying to evaluate agentic coding tools for internal pilots.

item.title: The Claude Code Source Map Leak: Clean Up Your Build Pipeline
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: An accidentally shipped source map exposed the bundled JavaScript behind Claude Code, including prompts and undisclosed product behaviors. Source maps in production remain a top OWASP-class leak.

item.title: AI Assistants Now Inject Ads. Audit Your Supply Chain.
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: A developer caught GitHub Copilot inserting promotional language for a third-party product into a code review comment. Treat AI assistant output as untrusted content in your supply chain.

item.title: Bot Detections Hidden Privacy Cost for SRQ SaaS Users
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: A reverse-engineering deep dive showed Cloudflare Turnstile inspecting browser internals and React state before letting users type into ChatGPT. Modern bot detection is far more invasive than CAPTCHAs.

item.title: Founder Succession Planning for Bradenton Small Businesses
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: GitLabs founder went public this week about his cancer diagnosis and his plan to keep founding companies. Its a hard reminder that founder/owner risk is a real continuity issue for SMBs.

item.title: Opt Out by April 24: GitHub Copilot Training on Private Repos
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: GitHub announced a policy change that defaults private repositories into Copilot training datasets unless users opt out before April 24, 2026. Audit your orgs settings now.

item.title: EU Rejects Chat Control: A Big Privacy Win, Briefly
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: In a dramatic vote, the EU Parliament rejected the Chat Control proposal that would have mandated client-side scanning of private messages. End-to-end encryption survives - for now.

item.title: Why Encryption Backdoors Break Security for SRQ Businesses
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: A campaign site lays out why scan only CSAM is technically impossible. Its also a great explainer to share with non-technical clients about why surveillance backdoors are a business risk.

item.title: Wine 11 Closes the Gap: Cheaper Workstation Refreshes for SMBs
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: Wine 11 introduces a kernel-assisted syscall translation layer that closes most of the gap with native Windows performance. For cost-conscious SRQ refresh cycles, that changes the math.

item.title: Data Sovereignty: When Florida Businesses Look to EU Hosting
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: A founder documented the practical steps of moving SaaS infrastructure and personal data out of US providers to EU equivalents. A useful reference for clients exploring data sovereignty.

item.title: Post-Git Version Control: Should SRQ Engineering Teams Care?
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: BitTorrent creator Bram Cohen lays out what should come after Git: better merge semantics, content-addressed history, and first-class large-file support. Heres how SRQ engineering leaders should react.

item.title: Ubuntu 26.04 Breaks 46 Years of Sudo Habit. Test Before Upgrade.
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: Ubuntu 26.04 changes sudo so password entry produces audible and visible feedback, breaking a Unix convention from 1980. Small UX changes in LTS distros can break ancient automation.

item.title: OpenCode and the Case for Open-Source AI in SRQ Workshops
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: OpenCode is a fully open-source alternative to Claude Code and Cursor with pluggable model backends. For shops that cannot ship code to closed AI vendors, its the most credible option to date.

item.title: When Beloved OSS Gets Acquired: Vendor Risk for Sarasota IT
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: Astral, the team behind ultra-fast Python tools uv and ruff, announced acquisition by OpenAI. The Python community is debating the long-term fate of these critical OSS tools.

item.title: Rob Pikes 1989 Rules That Still Drive Smart IT Decisions
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: Rob Pikes five rules - measure dont guess, fancy algorithms are slow on small n - resurfaced and dominated discussion this week. They still hold up 37 years later.

item.title: Style-Transfer LLMs Have Arrived. Now What for SRQ Marketing?
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: Kagi shipped a tongue-in-cheek translator that converts plain English into LinkedIn-flavored prose. Underneath the joke is a real demo of style-transfer LLM features SRQ marketing teams should know.

item.title: Prediction Markets, Information Warfare, and Your Brand Risk
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: A reporter described receiving death threats from Polymarket traders trying to manipulate his coverage. The piece exposes how prediction markets create perverse incentives around real-world reporting.

item.title: Cross-Border Data Rules: Why SRQ Companies Should Care
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: Canadian internet law expert Michael Geist breaks down Bill C-22, which still requires telcos and platforms to build interception capabilities. SRQ businesses with Canadian operations should track this.

item.title: Locked-Down Linux for Front-Desk and Kiosk Deployments
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: A new Linux distribution explicitly designed for non-technical users focuses on large fonts, minimal menus, and tamper-resistant defaults. A potential kiosk Linux candidate for SRQ deployments.

item.title: Can You Run AI On-Prem? A Practical Guide for Bradenton SMBs
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: A new tool detects your hardware and tells you which open-weight models you can realistically run locally. Hand this to any SRQ client asking about on-prem AI.

item.title: Breaking Free from Legacy Vendor Lock-In: Clean-Room Migrations
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: Malus is a new service offering on-demand clean-room reverse-engineering environments for teams reimplementing proprietary specs. A sign that legal-engineering hybrid services are productizing.

item.title: Drawing the Line on AI-Generated Content at Work
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: Hacker News updated its guidelines to explicitly prohibit AI-generated or AI-edited comments after a year of growing slop. Communities are pushing back. Whats your business policy?

item.title: Tony Hoares Lessons That Still Matter for SRQ IT Leaders
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: Tony Hoare - inventor of Quicksort, the null reference, and CSP - passed away at 91. The thread is a long retrospective on his influence and a reminder that the foundations are still young.

item.title: Are AI Coding Tools Really a Money Pit? A Sarasota Reality Check
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: A detailed analysis pushed back on viral claims that Anthropic loses thousands per Claude Code power user. The unit economics are usually better than they look.

item.title: Sandboxing AI Coding Agents: A Safer Default for SRQ Teams
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: Agent Safehouse is a new tool that wraps AI coding agents in macOS native sandboxing so they can edit files and run commands with strong isolation. Sandboxing AI agents is becoming standard practice.

item.title: A Decade of Docker: What Containers Mean for Bradenton SMBs
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: A retrospective in CACM on how containerization reshaped software delivery, with hard data on adoption and security incidents - and a look at what the next decade looks like.

item.title: Tech Layoffs Mean Better IT Hiring for Sarasota Businesses
item.author: hello@simpleitsrq.com (Dancho Ivanov)
item.description: BLS data shows tech-sector job losses now exceeding both the dot-com aftermath and the 2008 financial crisis. Heres what that means for SRQ businesses hiring IT talent.
